name: Wendell Brunious
abstract: Wendell Brunious (born October 27, 1954, New Orleans) is an American jazz trumpeter and bandleader. Born on October 27, 1954, Brunious was born into a Louisiana Creole family, the son of Nazimova "Chinee" Santiago and John "Picket" Brunious, Sr., a trumpeter who studied at Juilliard and played with the Onward Brass Band, Young Tuxedo Bra…

interviews.title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Barrett
about_quotes.evidence: My name is Dan Barrett and I’m privileged and honored to introduce a very good friend of mine and a terrific trumpet player from New Orleans, Mr. Wendell Brunious.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Barrett interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Evan Christopher
about_quotes.evidence: You interviewed Wendell Brunious, one of the New Orleans musicians that’s — I showed you that picture of him when he was like six or seven years old standing there with his sister watching... His father played in the Tuxedo band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Evan Christopher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 1, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: Wallace Davenport and Wendell Brunious, and a few others, Wynton Marsallis and his brother are playing or taking up, the young blacks are coming back again with jazz more in the forefront.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: I was talking with Wendell Brunious, a fabulous trumpet player, about that, how we have both gone through these stages.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999
